title: New York style giant chocolate chip cookies
subtitle: Large, crisp-edged, chewy-centred dark chocolate chip cookies.
excerpt: New York style cookies are large, thin at the edges, chewy in the middle, and full of dark chocolate. The dough is made with melted butter rather than creamed butter, which gives a flat, crispy edge. The cookies are big: each one uses about 80g of dough and bakes to roughly 12 cm across. Makes 12.
sourceNotes: American-style oversized cookies with crisp edges and gooey centres became widely known in Britain from the late 1990s onwards. The technique of browning the butter and using a high ratio of brown sugar to white gives the characteristic toffee depth. Resting the dough overnight in the fridge is a New York bakery practice associated with the Levain Bakery and similar; it dries the surface slightly so the cookie spreads less and develops a better crust. This recipe uses melted rather than browned butter for a faster result while keeping the characteristic flat-edged shape.

recipe.freezeNotes: Roll the dough into balls and freeze on a tray, then bag. Bake from frozen at 170°C fan for 16 to 18 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: The dough doubles easily and freezes well. Make a large batch and freeze unbaked balls.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough keeps in the fridge for up to 3 days; longer rest gives a more complex flavour and a crisper edge.

recipeTools.notes: Two large trays lined with baking paper.

content.text: These cookies use melted butter, not softened butter. Melted butter makes the dough thinner and less aerated, which means the cookies spread flat and develop crispy lacey edges while staying chewy in the centre. The dough needs 30 minutes in the fridge before baking so it firms up enough to hold its shape.

Whisk {{unsalted-butter}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, and {{caster-sugar}} together in a large bowl until combined. Add {{eggs}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and whisk until the mixture is smooth and slightly thickened.
Add {{plain-flour}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Stir with a wooden spoon or spatula until a thick dough forms. Fold in {{dark-chocolate}}.
Refrigerate the dough for at least 30 minutes (or up to 3 days for better flavour and texture).
Heat the oven to 170°C fan (190°C conventional). Line two large baking trays with baking paper.

Scoop the dough into 12 equal balls (roughly 80g each) and place well apart on the trays, at most 6 per tray. Flatten each ball slightly with your palm. Scatter a few sea salt flakes on top of each if using.

Bake one tray at a time for 12 to 14 minutes until the edges are set and golden but the centres still look underdone. They will firm up as they cool. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.

attrs.heading: Troubleshooting
attrs.intro: Cookie spread is the main variable to control.

items.symptom: Cookies spread too much and thin out.
items.cause: Dough too warm, or butter still hot when the eggs were added.
items.fix: Chill the dough for longer before baking. Make sure the melted butter has cooled before mixing.

items.symptom: Cookies solid all the way through with no chew.
items.fix: Pull them when the edges are set but the centre looks underdone. They continue cooking on the hot tray.
